gaps: No existing test case validates that the 'Apps' section appears in Device Settings for Voyager Focus 2 on iOS after pairing.
No existing test case covers the Device Settings screen structure/content validation for Voyager Focus 2 specifically.
No existing test case validates section-level UI elements (Apps, Audio, etc.) within Device Settings for any headset model.
No existing test case covers iOS-specific behavior of Device Settings sections for Voyager Focus 2.
No existing test case validates that all expected sections render correctly after the pairing flow completes.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-001: Apps Section Present in Device Settings — Voyager Focus 2 (iOS) — Post-Pairing Validation
steps: Precondition: Voyager Focus 2 is unpaired. Phone is iOS.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Pair Voyager Focus 2 via Bluetooth (Settings → Bluetooth → tap device).
3. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app → tap connected Voyager Focus 2 device card.
4. Navigate to: Device Settings (gear icon or settings row).
5. Observe all section headers visible on the Device Settings screen.
generated_testcases.expected: Device Settings screen displays the following sections in order: 'Apps', 'Audio', 'Headset Settings', 'More'. The 'Apps' section is present and rendered above 'Audio'. Actual (If Bug): 'Apps' section is missing entirely; only 'Audio', 'Headset Settings', and 'More' appear.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-002: Apps Section Content — Voyager Focus 2 (iOS) — Section Structure Validation
steps: Precondition: Voyager Focus 2 is paired and connected to iOS device.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 device card.
2. Navigate to: Device Settings.
3. Tap the 'Apps' section header to expand/collapse (if collapsible).
4. Observe the list of items/rows under the 'Apps' section.
5. Scroll the entire Device Settings screen and verify no other section contains app-related rows.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section contains at least one row (e.g., 'App Alerts' or similar app management option). No app-related rows appear under 'Audio', 'Headset Settings', or 'More' sections. All app functionality is scoped exclusively under the 'Apps' section header.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-003: Apps Section Persists After App Restart — Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Focus 2 is paired and connected to iOS device.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
2. Verify 'Apps' section is visible. Note its position and content.
3. Press Home button to background the app.
4. Force-kill the app (swipe up from app switcher).
5. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Tap Voyager Focus 2 device card → Device Settings.
7. Observe section headers.
generated_testcases.expected: 'Apps' section remains present in Device Settings after app force-kill and relaunch. Section order and content match pre-kill state. Actual (If Bug): 'Apps' section missing after app restart, indicating a lifecycle state loss bug.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-004: Apps Section Persists After Bluetooth Disconnect/Reconnect — Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Focus 2 is paired and connected to iOS device.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
2. Verify 'Apps' section is visible.
3. Turn off Voyager Focus 2 headset (power slider OFF).
4. Wait 10 seconds. Observe app connection state (should show 'Disconnected').
5. Turn on Voyager Focus 2 headset. Wait for auto-reconnect.
6. Tap device card → Device Settings.
7. Observe section headers.
generated_testcases.expected: 'Apps' section remains present in Device Settings after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnection cycle. Actual (If Bug): 'Apps' section missing after reconnect, indicating the section is not re-initialized on reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-005: Apps Section Not Present for Non-Voyager Focus 2 Devices (iOS Negative Test)
steps: Precondition: A non-Voyager Focus 2 headset (e.g., Voyager 4220, Voyager Free 60+) is paired and connected to iOS device.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the non-Focus 2 device card.
2. Navigate to: Device Settings.
3. Observe all section headers on the screen.
4. Repeat steps 1-3 for at least 2 different non-Focus 2 models.
generated_testcases.expected: For non-Voyager Focus 2 devices, the 'Apps' section is NOT present in Device Settings. Only 'Audio', 'Headset Settings', and 'More' sections appear. This confirms the 'Apps' section is model-specific to Voyager Focus 2 and not a universal rendering bug.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-006: Apps Section Visible Immediately After Pairing Completion — Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Focus 2 is in pairing mode. iOS Bluetooth settings are open.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app (fresh launch, no cached device).
2. Pair Voyager Focus 2 via in-app pairing flow (or system Bluetooth → return to app).
3. IMMEDIATELY after pairing completes and device shows as 'Connected', tap the device card.
4. Navigate to: Device Settings.
5. Observe section headers without any delay or navigation away.
generated_testcases.expected: 'Apps' section is visible immediately upon first entry to Device Settings after pairing completes. No additional refresh, navigation away, or app restart is required for the section to render. Actual (If Bug): 'Apps' section absent on first entry, only appearing after a subsequent app restart or reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-APPS-007: Device Settings Section Order Consistency — Voyager Focus 2 (iOS) — UI Layout Validation
steps: Precondition: Voyager Focus 2 is paired and connected to iOS device.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
2. Capture screenshot of the full Device Settings screen.
3. Note the vertical order of all section headers from top to bottom.
4. Navigate back to device list, then re-enter Device Settings.
5. Repeat step 2 and compare section order.
6. Repeat steps 1-5 across 3 separate app sessions (force-kill between each).
generated_testcases.expected: Section order is consistent across all entries: 'Apps' (top), 'Audio', 'Headset Settings', 'More' (bottom). No sections are reordered, duplicated, or missing across sessions. The 'Apps' section is always the first section in Device Settings for Voyager Focus 2 on iOS.
